title: Box Breathing
description: Breathe in for 4 counts, hold for 4, breathe out for 4, hold for 4. Repeat 4 times.

title: Progressive Muscle Relaxation
description: Starting from your toes, tense each muscle group for 5 seconds then release. Work up to your face. Releases physical tension held in the body.

title: 10-Minute Walk Outside
description: Leave your space and walk outside without your phone. Even a short walk in daylight reduces cortisol and resets your nervous system.

title: 5-4-3-2-1 Grounding
description: Name 5 things you can see, 4 you can touch, 3 you can hear, 2 you can smell, 1 you can taste. Brings you back into the present moment.

title: Timed Free Write
description: Write without stopping for 5 minutes — no editing, no judgement. Get everything out of your head and onto the page.

title: Brain Dump
description: Write down every single thing that is in your head right now — tasks, worries, thoughts, feelings. Getting it out of your head reduces cognitive load.

title: Draw How You Feel
description: Draw, scribble, or doodle how you feel right now — no artistic skill required. Externalising emotion through drawing reduces its intensity.

title: Tear Up Paper
description: Take a piece of paper or a magazine and tear it up as aggressively as you need to. Physical release of anger without physical harm.

title: Build a Feelings Playlist
description: Open Spotify or YouTube and build a playlist that matches exactly how you feel right now — not how you want to feel. Let the music meet you where you are.

title: Worst / Best / Most Likely
description: Write the worst case scenario, the best case, and the most realistic outcome. Most anxiety lives in the gap between worst case and most likely.

title: Text One Person
description: Text one person something real — not 'hey' but something specific. Connection does not require a full conversation.

title: Tidy One Small Thing
description: Clear just one surface — your desk, your bedside table, the kitchen counter. Environmental order creates a small but real sense of control.

title: Watch Something Natural
description: Open a window and watch the sky, trees, or street for 5 minutes without your phone. Passive attention to the natural world reduces cortisol.

title: Cold Water on Your Face
description: Splash cold water on your face or hold your wrists under cold running water for 30 seconds. Triggers the dive reflex and slows heart rate fast.
